info.title: Microsoft Graph API - Outlook
info.description: Microsoft Graph API provides unified access to Microsoft 365 Outlook services including mail, calendar, contacts, and mailbox settings. This spec covers the Outlook-related subset of Microsoft Graph v1.0 endpoints.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description: Read, create, send, reply, forward, and manage email messages

tags.name: Mail Folders
tags.description: Manage mail folders (Inbox, Sent Items, custom folders, etc.)

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Create, read, update, and delete calendar events

tags.name: Attachments
tags.description: Manage file and item attachments on messages and events

tags.name: Mailbox Settings
tags.description: Get and update mailbox settings including automatic replies and time zone

get.description: Get the messages in the signed-in user's mailbox (including the Deleted Items and Clutter folders).

post.description: Create a draft of a new message in the user's mailbox.

get.description: Retrieve the properties and relationships of a message object.

patch.description: Update the properties of a message object.

delete.description: Delete a message in the specified user's mailbox.

post.description: Send the message specified in the request body. The message is saved in the Sent Items folder by default.

saveToSentItems.description: Indicates whether to save the message in Sent Items.

post.description: Reply to the sender of a message, add a comment, and send.

comment.description: A comment to include with the reply.

post.description: Reply to all recipients of a message.
Forward a message to specified recipients.

post.summary: Move a message to a folder
post.description: Move a message to a specified folder.

destinationId.description: The destination folder ID or well-known folder name.

get.description: Retrieve a list of attachment objects attached to a message.

post.summary: Add an attachment to a message
post.description: Add a file or item attachment to a message.

get.description: Read the properties, relationships, or raw contents of an attachment.

delete.description: Delete an attachment from a message.

get.description: Get the mail folder collection directly under the root folder of the signed-in user.

post.description: Create a new mail folder under the root folder of the user's mailbox.

get.description: Retrieve the properties and relationships of a mail folder object.

patch.description: Update the properties of a mail folder object.

delete.description: Delete a mail folder and its contents.

get.summary: List messages in a mail folder
get.description: Get all the messages in the specified mail folder.
Get the folder collection under the specified folder.
Get all the user's calendars.

post.description: Create a new calendar for the user.

get.description: Get the properties and relationships of a calendar object.

patch.description: Update the properties of a calendar object.

delete.description: Delete a calendar other than the default calendar.

get.description: Get the user's events from the default calendar or a specified calendar.

post.description: Create a new event in the user's default calendar.

get.description: Get the properties and relationships of the specified event object.

patch.description: Update the properties of the specified event object.

delete.description: Delete an event from the user's calendar.

post.description: Accept the specified event in the user's calendar.
Decline invitation to the specified event in the user's calendar.
Tentatively accept the specified event in the user's calendar.

get.description: Retrieve a list of attachment objects attached to an event.

post.summary: Add an attachment to an event
post.description: Add a file or item attachment to an event.

get.description: Retrieve a list of events in a specified calendar.

post.summary: Create an event in a calendar
post.description: Create a new event in the specified calendar.

get.description: Get the occurrences, exceptions, and single instances of events in a calendar view defined by a time range.

parameters.name: startDateTime
parameters.description: The start date and time of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description: The end date and time of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format.

get.description: Get a contact collection from the default contacts folder of the signed-in user.

post.description: Add a contact to the root contacts folder or to the contacts endpoint of another contact folder.

get.description: Retrieve the properties and relationships of a contact object.

patch.description: Update the properties of a contact object.

get.description: Get the contact folder collection in the root folder of the signed-in user.

post.description: Create a new contact folder under the user's default contact folder.

get.description: Get a contact folder by using the contact folder ID.

patch.description: Update the properties of a contact folder object.

get.description: Get the contacts in a specified contact folder.
Get the user's mailbox settings including automatic replies, locale, time zone, and working hours.

patch.description: Update one or more settings for the user's mailbox.

TopParam.name: $top
TopParam.description: Sets the page size of results.

SkipParam.name: $skip
SkipParam.description: Skips the specified number of results.

SelectParam.name: $select
SelectParam.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response.

SearchParam.name: $search
SearchParam.description: Returns results based on search criteria.

MessageIdParam.name: message_id
MessageIdParam.description: The unique identifier of the message.

FolderIdParam.name: folder_id
FolderIdParam.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder, or a well-known folder name (inbox, drafts, sentitems, deleteditems).

CalendarIdParam.name: calendar_id
CalendarIdParam.description: The unique identifier of the calendar.

EventIdParam.name: event_id
EventIdParam.description: The unique identifier of the event.

ContactIdParam.name: contact_id
ContactIdParam.description: The unique identifier of the contact.

AttachmentIdParam.name: attachment_id
AttachmentIdParam.description: The unique identifier of the attachment.

ContactFolderIdParam.name: contact_folder_id
ContactFolderIdParam.description: The unique identifier of the contact folder.

name.description: The display name of the person or entity.

address.description: The email address of the person or entity.

content.description: The content of the item body.

dateTime.description: A single point of time in a combined date and time representation (e.g., 2024-01-01T12:00:00).

timeZone.description: Represents a time zone (e.g., Pacific Standard Time).

displayName.description: The name associated with the location.

interval.description: The number of units between occurrences.

@odata.nextLink.description: URL to get the next page of results.

displayName.description: The display name of the folder.

isHidden.description: Indicates whether the folder is hidden.

sendResponse.description: Whether a response is sent to the organizer.

@odata.type.description: The OData type of the attachment (#microsoft.graph.fileAttachment or #microsoft.graph.itemAttachment).

name.description: The display name of the attachment.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the attachment.

size.description: The size in bytes of the attachment.

isInline.description: Set to true if the attachment is an inline attachment.

contentBytes.description: The base64-encoded contents of the file (for file attachments).

contentId.description: The ID of the attachment in the Exchange store.

name.description: The display name of the attachment.

externalAudience.description: The audience external to the user's organization who will receive the external reply message.

internalReplyMessage.description: The automatic reply to send to internal audience.

externalReplyMessage.description: The automatic reply to send to external audience.

startTime.description: The time of the day that the user starts working.

endTime.description: The time of the day that the user stops working.

timeZone.description: The default time zone for the user's mailbox.

archiveFolder.description: Folder ID of the archive folder for the user.

BadRequest.description: Bad request - the request was malformed or contained invalid parameters.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - authentication is required or the provided token is invalid or expired.

oauth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authorization using Microsoft identity platform.

scopes.Mail.ReadWrite: Read and write access to user mail
scopes.Calendars.ReadWrite: Have full access to user calendars
scopes.Contacts.ReadWrite: Have full access to user contacts
scopes.MailboxSettings.ReadWrite: Read and write user mailbox settings
scopes.User.Read: Sign in and read user profile

bearerAuth.description: Bearer token obtained via OAuth 2.0 authorization.
